payload.title: Gate readiness on database availability with minimal behavioral risk
payload.approach: Update only readiness logic and its tests, explicitly preserving startup/liveness contracts and verifying behavior through existing build and test scripts.
payload.rationale: A risk-first approach limits blast radius by changing one readiness signal, hardening failure handling, and using existing verification gates to prevent regressions.

acceptanceCoverage.strategy: Modify `HealthService.readiness()` to require both process readiness and `database.isAvailable()` success before returning true.
Wrap DB availability checks in guarded async error handling that maps any rejection to false readiness and does not throw from the probe path.
Leave `liveness()` and `startup()` implementations untouched to preserve external contracts unless a direct dependency on readiness is required by failure semantics.
Add unit tests in `test/health-service.test.ts` for readiness false on DB unavailability and true when DB is available while process state is running.
Run and pass both `npm run build` and `npm test` as the repository already defines them.

invariantProtection.strategy: Keep interface and method signatures in `src/health-service.ts` and related test doubles unchanged so existing callers remain source-compatible.
Avoid new dependencies and compile with strict typing by using existing TypeScript constructs and interfaces.
Restrict behavioral changes to readiness gating only and do not alter liveness/startup or unrelated deployment probe configuration.
Only read `deployment.json` for context unless an explicit approval request arises to edit probe settings.

files.purpose: Implement defensive DB-aware readiness logic while preserving process-state compatibility
Add/adjust probe tests covering DB available/unavailable and error rejection behavior
Read-only validation of readiness probe target alignment with `/health/ready`

steps.description: Review current implementations in `src/health-service.ts` and `test/health-service.test.ts` to confirm current probe behavior and the mutable DB test double contract.
Implement readiness to short-circuit on process-not-ready before calling DB, then include async DB availability check and map failures to false with explicit error-tolerant handling.
Add tests for false readiness when DB returns false and when DB `isAvailable()` rejects, plus positive path when DB is true and process is ready.
Run required validation commands and, only if needed, verify `deployment.json` still points readiness to `/health/ready` without edits.
Document residual risk and rollout guidance if readiness flap or transient DB latency is observed.

verificationCommands.name: Test
verificationCommands.purpose: Run full test suite including new readiness DB scenarios

risks: Readiness may flap during transient DB blips, increasing rolling restart frequency if probe thresholds are too strict.
Synchronous startup assumptions can fail under prolonged DB outages, potentially delaying rollout while pods wait for readiness recovery.
Error-path behavior in `isAvailable()` could accidentally mask deeper dependency failures if logging/observability is insufficient.
assumptions: `/health/ready` is the routing gate the platform uses for pod readiness decisions.
Process state methods remain stable and do not already encode DB semantics.

unknowns.description: Whether readiness failures on `/health/ready` are strictly enforced by all upstream traffic routers is not verifiable from this repository alone.
unknowns.resolution: Validate in staging/infrastructure runbook after rollout against actual ingress/service-mesh behavior.

unknowns.description: Whether there is additional routing logic outside this repo that overrides readiness signaling cannot be confirmed in this code surface.
unknowns.resolution: Inspect deployment pipeline and platform config for any external traffic-gating overrides before wider rollout.

recovery: Revert only `src/health-service.ts` and `test/health-service.test.ts` changes if readiness impact exceeds acceptance thresholds and redeploy with prior test/build behavior.
